title: NADE's Top Issues for 2025
description: A 2-page advocacy position paper from the National Association of Disability Examiners (NADE) listing its top policy priorities for 2025 affecting the state Disability Determination Services (DDS). Issues raised include: Recruitment and Retention (continued funding to maintain adequate DDS staffing, more hiring-authority flexibility, telework, competitive salaries); Training (funding for new and ongoing examiner training and nationally uniform materials); Claimant Communication (email/text messaging, the emailer pilot, document uploading); Evaluation of Vocational Regulations and Processes; Disability Examiner Decision Authority (DEDA) expansion; Customer Service Improvements; IT Modernization (IMAGEN expansion, Electronic Records Express); Cooperative Disability Investigation (CDI) unit support; and re-examination of the Medical Improvement Review Standard (MIRS). Authored within NADE (PDF author metadata: Garcia, Marjorie E., DDS Salem).
geographic_coverage: United States (national DDS / SSA disability program)
notes: Primary advocacy/position document published by NADE, hence Authoritative. Real metadata and full issue list extracted from both pages (PDF CreationDate 2025-04-02). No sibling .source.txt; exact source URL unknown, so nade.org canonical domain used (deep link not invented).
